text.title: surveys Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns repeatedly observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Item 'How would you rate the speed and accuracy?' yields uninterpretable mean.
antipattern.root-cause: Two attributes conflated in one item.
antipattern.fix: Split into two single-attribute items. Validator flags 'and' connectors in items.

antipattern.symptom: Item 'How amazing is the new feature?' produces inflated positives.
antipattern.fix: Replace with neutral 'How would you rate the new feature on a 1-5 scale?'.

antipattern.symptom: 1-5 scale with only endpoints labeled; midpoints interpreted differently across respondents.
After data collection, analyst slices by 12 dimensions until 'a significant difference' appears; replication fails.
antipattern.fix: Pre-register cross-tabs + weights + significance tests before fielding; mark post-hoc analyses exploratory.
